docs.text: Graphic Server Protocol The Graphic Server Protocol (GSP) defines a protocol used between a data visualization library and a server that executes low-level graphic commands. Overview The protocol defines the format of the messages sent using JSON-RPC between the dataviz library and the graphics server. Specifications This document describes the protocol in every details such as to allow new implementations, server or client side. Implementations Matplotlib (Python/ Antigrain ) Datoviz (C++/ Vulkan ) pygfx (Python/ WGPU ) Warning This is a work in progress. The protocol is still in early alpha phase and specifications are subject to changes.
docs.title: Graphic Server Protocol

docs.text: Capabilities Core Canvas alpha 2D drawing areas, onscreen or offscreen. Viewport alpha Subdivision of a canvas Buffer alpha Arbitrary one-dimensional data representation Visuals Points alpha Collection of points with arbitrary size, edge color, edge thickness and fill color. Markers alpha Collection of pre-defined markers with arbitrary size, edge color, edge thickness and fill color. Segments alpha Collection of anti-aliased line segments with arbitrary thickness, color and pre-defined end caps. Polylines alpha Collection of anti-aliased polylines with arbitrary thickness, color and pre-defined joins and end caps. Polygons alpha Collection of anti-aliased polygons with arbitrary edge and fill color, edge tickness and pre-defined joins. Meshes alpha Collection of meshes with arbitrary fill color. Volumes alpha Collection of volumes Text alpha Collection of text strings Transforms Linear transforms alpha Zoom, translate and rotations (2D/3D) Colormaps alpha Arbitrary color mapping from a scale Projections alpha Arbitrary data projections
docs.title: Capabilities

Volumes alpha Collection of volumes Text alpha Collection of text strings

docs.text: How it works?
docs.title: How it works?

docs.text: Rationale If you look closely at a scientific figure ( Fig. 1 ), you might observe that it is actually made of a (large) set of elementary components, namely points (or markers), lines, glyphs (text) and polygons ( Fig. 2 ) Glyphs, markers and polygons can be further decomposed into triangles such that the elementary components are really points, lines and triangles. When designing a scientific figure, you hardly manipulate these components explicitely. Instead, you use high-level functions that ultimately produce these elementary components. Some of these high-level functions can produce all the elements at once while some other allows you to specify only a subset of these elements. Figure 1 A regular scatter plot as it could be created by a number of scientific libraries, with probably some variations in style. What defines a scientific visualization library is the choice and the definition of these high-level functions. You can choose to have only very high-level functions that allow a user to quickly design a figure (e.g. ggplot , vega , seaborn ) or you can give user more control by providing mid-level functions that allows to further tune the figure (e.g. matplotlib , vispy ). In the most extreme case, you might decide to give user a total freedom at the price of complexity ( TikZ ). There is no definitive API because some users will enjoy the magic of a high-level interface that takes most decisions for them, while some other users will want to have a fine control of pretty much everything. Figure 2 The figure 1 above can be decomposed in a set of markers, lines and glyphs. From a developper point of view, independently of the actual library, the task remains the same: the library needs to draw points, lines, markers, glyphs, polygons and meshes, taking advantage of some lower-level library or doing everything itself. Ultimately, this represent a lot of redundant efforts across libraries. The graphic server protocol is a proposal to mutualize efforts across libraries, languages and platforms .
docs.title: Rationale

You can choose to have only very high-level functions that allow a user to quickly design a figure (e.g. ggplot , vega , seaborn ) or you can give user more control by providing mid-level functions that allows to further tune the figure (e.g. matplotlib , vispy ). Ultimately, this represent a lot of redundant efforts across libraries. The graphic server protocol is a proposal to mutualize efforts across libraries, languages and platforms .
docs.title: Rationale

docs.text: Base protocol Header part Content part Contains the actual content of the message. The content part of a message uses JSON-RPC to describe requests, responses and notifications. The content part is encoded using the charset provided in the Content-Type field. It defaults to utf-8, which is the only encoding supported right now. If a server or client receives a header with a different encoding than utf-8 it should respond with an error. Example Co ntent - Le n g t h : ...\r\ n \r\ n { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas" , "params" : { ... } } Base types Request message Response message Notification message
docs.title: Base protocol

docs.text: Contains the actual content of the message. The content part of a message uses JSON-RPC to describe requests, responses and notifications. The content part is encoded using the charset provided in the Content-Type field. It defaults to utf-8, which is the only encoding supported right now. If a server or client receives a header with a different encoding than utf-8 it should respond with an error. Example Co ntent - Le n g t h : ...\r\ n \r\ n { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas" , "params" : { ... } }

docs.title: Structures

docs.text: Buffer ( count , dtype , data ) Request the creation of a uni-dimensional buffer with count elements of type dtype and data content. Python JSON-RPC import struct data = [ 0 , 0 , 1 , 1 , 2 , 2 ] data = struct . pack ( "f" * len ( data ), * data ) vec3 = Datatype ( "f:x/f:y" ) buffer = Buffer ( 3 , vec3 , data ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Buffer" , "params" : { "size" : 100 , "dtype" : 1 , "data" : "AAAAAAAAAAAAIA/AACAPwAAAEAAAABA" } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 3 Parameters Name Type Description count int Number of elements dtype Datatype Element datatype data bytes Content of the buffer Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object Future Arrays can be partitionned
docs.title: Buffer

docs.text: BufferView ( buffer , key ) Partial view of an existing buffer indexed by key . Python JSON-RPC import struct data = [ 0 , 0 , 1 , 1 , 2 , 2 ] data = struct . pack ( "f" * len ( data ), * data ) vec3 = Datatype ( "f:x/f:y" ) buffer = Buffer ( 3 , vec3 , data ) view = BufferView ( buffer , "x" ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "BufferView" , "params" : { "buffer" : 1 , "key" : "x" , } } Parameters Name Type Description buffer Buffer Buffer to take view from key str Field to be accessed Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object Future No dicussion for the time being.
docs.title: Buffer view

docs.text: Canvas ( width , height , dpi , dpr , offscreen ) Request the creation of a two-dimensional drawing area (that can be offscreen ) of size width × height logical pixels using specified dpi (dots per inch) and dpr (device pixel ratio). The resulting canvas uses a standard color space ( sRGBA ) with at least 8 bits per channel. Python JSON-RPC Result canvas = Canvas ( 800 , 400 , 100 , 2 , True ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas" , "params" : { "width" : 800 , "height" : 400 , "dpi" : 100 , "dpr" : 2 , "offscreen" : false } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 5 Parameters Name Type Description width int Width of the drawing area in pixels. → width must be striclty greater than zero. height int Height of the drawing area in pixels. → height must be striclty greater than zero. dpi float Dots per inch → dpi must be striclty greater than zero. dpr float Device pixel ratio → dpr must be striclty greater than zero. offscreen bool Flag indicatng whether canvas is offscreen or not → offscreen can only be set at creation time. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value Future No dicussion at the moment. set_size ( width , height ) Set a new size for the canvas. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 16 , 8 , 100 , 2 , True ) canvas . set_size ( 16 , 16 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 2 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas/set_size" , "params" : { "id" : 1 , "width" : 16 , "height" : 8 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 2 Parameters Name Type Description width int Width of the canvas in logical pixels. → width must be striclty greater than zero. height int Height of the canvas in logical pixels. → height must be striclty greater than zero. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object set_dpi ( dpi ) Set the dots per inch. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 16 , 8 , 100 , 2 , True ) canvas . set_dpi ( 100 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 2 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas/set_dpi" , "params" : { "id" : 1 , "dpi" : 100 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 1 Parameters Name Type Description dpi float Dots per inch → dpi must be striclty greater than zero. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object set_dpr ( dpr ) Set the device pixel ratio. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 16 , 8 , 100 , 2 , True ) canvas . set_dpr ( 100 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 2 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Canvas/set_dpi" , "params" : { "id" : 1 , "dpr" : 100 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 1 Parameters Name Type Description dpr float Device pixel ratio → dpr must be striclty greater than zero. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object
docs.title: Canvas

docs.text: Datatype ( dtype ) Datatype describes the structure of a chunk of memory. Python JSON-RPC >>> vec2 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y" ) # 8 bytes total >>> Command . commands [ - 1 ] . to_json () { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Datatype" , "params" : { "type" : "f:x / f:y" , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 1 Parameters Name Type Description dtype str Description of the type (as a / separated list of atomic items) → type must fit datatype description. The description of the type is a / separated list of atomic items of the form type:[name]:[count] / … / type:[name]:[count] where name is a valid identifier, count is a strictly positive integer and type is one of: Type Alias Description Size Comment bool ? boolean 8 bits byte b signed integer 8 bits short s signed integer 16 bits int i signed integer 32 bits ubyte B unsigned integer 8 bits ushort S unsigned integer 16 bits uint I unsigned integer 32 bits half e half-precision floating-point number 16 bits 5 bits exponent, 10 bits mantissa float f single-precision floating-point number 32 bits 8 bits exponent, 23 bits mantissa double d double-precision floating-point number 64 bits 11 bits exponent, 52 bits mantissa datetime Y date/time representation 64 bits Offset from 1970-01-01T00:00:00 A type can be matched against a regular expression: r "(?P<type>[?bsiBSIefdY]):(?P<name>[A-Za-z0-9]*):(?P<count>[0-9]+)" Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value Future No dicussion for the time being. Examples vec2 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y" ) vec3 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y / f:z" ) vec4 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y / f:z / f:w" ) mat3x3 = Datatype ( "f::9" ) mat4x4 = Datatype ( "f::16" )
docs.title: Datatype

docs.text: Datatype describes the structure of a chunk of memory. Python JSON-RPC >>> vec2 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y" ) # 8 bytes total >>> Command . commands [ - 1 ] . to_json () { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Datatype" , "params" : { "type" : "f:x / f:y" , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 1 Parameters Name Type Description dtype str Description of the type (as a / separated list of atomic items) → type must fit datatype description. The description of the type is a / separated list of atomic items of the form type:[name]:[count] / … / type:[name]:[count] where name is a valid identifier, count is a strictly positive integer and type is one of: Type Alias Description Size Comment bool ? boolean 8 bits byte b signed integer 8 bits short s signed integer 16 bits int i signed integer 32 bits ubyte B unsigned integer 8 bits ushort S unsigned integer 16 bits uint I unsigned integer 32 bits half e half-precision floating-point number 16 bits 5 bits exponent, 10 bits mantissa float f single-precision floating-point number 32 bits 8 bits exponent, 23 bits mantissa double d double-precision floating-point number 64 bits 11 bits exponent, 52 bits mantissa datetime Y date/time representation 64 bits Offset from 1970-01-01T00:00:00 A type can be matched against a regular expression: r "(?P<type>[?bsiBSIefdY]):(?P<name>[A-Za-z0-9]*):(?P<count>[0-9]+)" Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value Future No dicussion for the time being. Examples vec2 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y" ) vec3 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y / f:z" ) vec4 = Datatype ( "f:x / f:y / f:z / f:w" ) mat3x3 = Datatype ( "f::9" ) mat4x4 = Datatype ( "f::16" )
docs.title: Datatype

docs.text: Viewport ( canvas , x , y , width , height ) Request the creation of a two-dimensional viewport at x , y coordinates with size equal to width × height logical pixels. Viewport needs to be fully contained inside the canvas. When several viewports are created, they are rendered in the order of their declaration. Python JSON-RPC Result canvas = Canvas ( 800 , 400 , 100 , 1 , False ) viewport = Viewport ( canvas , 100 , 100 , 300 , 200 , 1 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Viewport" , "params" : { "canvas" : 1 , "x" : 100 , "y" : 100 , "width" : 300 , "height" : 200 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 5 Parameters Name Type Description canvas Canvas Canvas where to create the viewport → canvas must have been created x int X coordinate of the viewport bottom left corner → Assert 0 <= x < canvas.width - 1 y int Y coordinate of the viewport bottom left corner → Assert 0 <= y < canvas.height - 1 width int Width of the viewport in logical pixels. → Assert 0 < width <= canvas.width - x height int Height of the viewport in logical pixels. → Assert 0 < height <= canvas.height - y Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object Future Viewports can be equipped with a rendering order Viewports can be equipped with a transform set_position ( x , y ) Set a new position x , y for the viewport bottom left corner. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 200 , 200 , 100 , 1 , False ) viewport = Viewport ( canvas , 10 , 10 , 100 , 100 , 1 ) viewport . set_position ( 0 , 0 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Viewport/set_position" , "params" : { "id" : 2 , "x" : 0 , "y" : 0 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 3 Parameters Name Type Description x int X coordinate of the bottom left corner y int Y coordinate of the bottom left corner Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object set_size ( width , height ) Set a new size witdh × height for the viewport. Note Coordinates can be negative or beyond canvas limits such that a viewport can be fully outside the canvas area and won't be rendered. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 200 , 200 , 100 , 1 , False ) viewport = Viewport ( canvas , 10 , 10 , 100 , 100 , 1 ) viewport . set_size ( 50 , 50 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Viewport/set_size" , "params" : { "id" : 2 , "width" : 50 , "height" : 50 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 3 Parameters Name Type Description width int Width of the viewport in logical pixels. height int Height of the viewport in logical pixels. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object
docs.title: Viewport

docs.title: set_position()

docs.text: Set a new size witdh × height for the viewport. Note Coordinates can be negative or beyond canvas limits such that a viewport can be fully outside the canvas area and won't be rendered. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 200 , 200 , 100 , 1 , False ) viewport = Viewport ( canvas , 10 , 10 , 100 , 100 , 1 ) viewport . set_size ( 50 , 50 ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Viewport/set_size" , "params" : { "id" : 2 , "width" : 50 , "height" : 50 , } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling UPDATE 3 Parameters Name Type Description width int Width of the viewport in logical pixels. height int Height of the viewport in logical pixels. Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object
docs.title: set_size()

docs.text: Pixels ( viewport , vertices , colors ) Request the creation several colored pixels whose positions are given by vertices and colors by `colors. Python JSON-RPC canvas = Canvas ( 512 , 512 , 100 , 1 , False ) viewport = Viewport ( canvas , 0 , 0 , 512 , 512 ) vertices = Buffer . from_numpy ( np . random . uniform ( 0 , 512 , ( 100 , 2 ))) colors = Buffer . from_numpy ( np . random . uniform ( 0 , 512 , ( 100 , 4 ))) pixels = Pixels ( viewport , vertices , colors ) { "jsonrpc" : "2.0" , "id" : 1 , "timestamp" : 0 , "method" : "Pixels" , "params" : { "viewport" : 2 , "vertices" : 4 , "colors" : 6 } } Protocol Request type # Parameters Asynchronous Error handling CREATE 3 Parameters Name Type Description viewport Viewport Viewport to use to display pixels vertices Buffer Pixel positions as (x,y,z) colors Buffer Pixel colors as (r,g,b,a) Errors INVALID_REQUEST : Request is malformed INVALID_METHOD : Method does not exist or is not available. INVALID_PARAMS : A parameter is missing or has an illegal value INVALID_OBJECT : A parameter refers to a non-existent object Future No discussion for the time being.
